entry.title: Re: Bringing Apps Onto SAP Fiori Launchpad - SAP S/4HANA User Experience
entry.content:
Hello Costiantyn,
good news: yes, there is a tool for migrating technical catalogs created with Launchpad Designer to technical catalogs that can be managed with Launchpad App Manager. You can find a how-to description in the blog post SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migr... - SAP Community by Jocelyn Dart.
best regards
Ruth

entry.title: Re: Setup Fund Management in SAP Public Cloud Grow Step b...
entry.content:
Hello @iRzSAP 

Thank you for your question.

To implement SAP Funds Management (FM) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (GROW), please note that FM is not available as a standard scope item in the Public Cloud Edition. It is only supported in SAP S/4HANA on-premises and Private Cloud Edition.

What to check:

  1. Confirm Edition – Verify if you are on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (GROW). If so, FM functionality is not supported.
  2. Alternative Approach – For budgeting and cost control in Public Cloud, consider using:
  • Cost Center Budgeting (via app: Manage Cost Center Budgets)
  • Availability Control for cost elements
  • Project System (PS) for investment tracking

Refer to SAP Help Portal:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Budget Availability Control

If FM is a critical requirement, a move to Private Cloud Edition may be necessary.

Let me know if you'd like further information

Best regards,

Chuma

entry.title: Workaround for SAM that is not working as expected after a new client version
entry.content:
Aa a workaround we need to go back to SSAM previous 2405 version.

Please, could you let us know the standard steps to use a previous SSAM version and if this procedure is going to "freeze" any further updates ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Workaround for SAM that is not working as expected af...
entry.content:
Hello,

When you are using the out-of-box SSAM Client App, you can just deploy the the 2405 App metadata version to your Mobile Services endpoint using BAS / WebIDE or Visual Studio Code.

Refer to the following product documentation pages:

https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/sap-service-and-asset-manager-installation-guide/downloading-metadata?version=2405&locale=en-US
https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/sap-service-and-asset-manager-installation-guide/import-project-in-sap-business-application-studio?version=2405&locale=en-US
https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/sap-service-and-asset-manager-installation-guide/deploying-project?version=2405&locale=en-US

You need to download the Metadata from the SAP Software Center and upload it so you can deploy it. Please see: 

Remember that, when you do the first synchronization on Version 2405 you may get an error asking to update your App. To fix this you will need to create your own client for distribution. For steps please see:

https://help.sap.com/docs/service-asset-manager/sap-service-and-asset-manager-installation-guide/sap-service-and-asset-manager-client-setup?version=2405&locale=en-US

About the updates question: each SSAM version will operate in parallel as an independent App on the system. So you control the metadata changes.
You also need to pay attention to the MDK version changes and compatibility, see: 2933065 - SAP Service and Asset Manager - MDK Support Matrix

Hope this helps.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: HCM Brasil: Crédito do Trabalhador - eConsignado
entry.content:
@leandrodavila D´Avila, tudo bem?

Diante das últimas portarias divulgadas pela governo, eles ainda não disponibilizaram de fato os leiautes consolidados para que a SAP disponibilize um programa de importação desses arquivos dos empréstimos do Crédito Justo, para o novo infotipo desta solução eConsignado. Pergunto se, nos testes que vocês participaram como GT-Piloto, esses arquivos ainda sofrerão alterações, para que vocês possam divulgar uma data prevista para a entrega desta solução Standard aos clientes.

Obrigado.

https://www.gov.br/trabalho-e-emprego/pt-br/assuntos/credito-do-trabalhador/legislacao-do-governo-federal/

entry.title: Re: How can we send an automatic notification to the cust...
entry.content:
Hello @Ingrid_at_Applium 

Thank you for your question.

In the standard functionality of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, there is currently no out-of-the-box feature that sends automatic reminder emails to customers for expiring service quotations.

However, here are a couple of options to achieve it;

Option 1: Use Situation Handling (Recommended if available in your scope)
App: Manage Situation Types

You can define a situation scenario that monitors the expiration date of service quotations.

Configure it to trigger an internal or external notification a few days before the expiration.

Note: This requires the right business context (e.g., Service Quotation) and scope item.

Option 2: Custom Email Notification via Custom Logic
App: Custom Logic – You can implement a BAdI (e.g., SD_QUOTATION_EXPIRY_NOTIFICATION) if available.

This would allow you to define logic that triggers an email based on quotation expiration date.

Combine with an Output Management template for email formatting.

Option 3: Develop a Scheduled Job (as last resort)
Create a custom CDS view to list expiring quotations.

Use Email Templates + Scheduled Job to send out notifications via Communication Arrangements.

Let me know if you need further details,

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Service Quotation in SAP S4 HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
First of all, thanks a lot for your help!

Can you share an SS of the app in the system?

I type exactly the name and I can´t find it.

entry.title: Re: Logs to check who deployed/undeployed the iFlow
entry.content:
Hello,

Indeed in CPI monitor page we can only see who deployed it.

You will be able to find the user information in case of undeployment inside the Audit Logs following the steps bellow:

  1. Open the BTP Cockpit.
  2. In the left menu, go to Services -> Instances and Subscriptions.
  3. Under the Subscriptions tab, locate the Audit Log Viewer Service.
  4. Open the Audit Log Viewer Service to access the Audit Log.
  5. You should see entries that include:
    • User ID / Email of the person who performed the action.
    • Timestamp.
    • Action (e.g., undeploy).

Regards,

Suelen

entry.title: [BYD] Modify Picking List Sorting criteria
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

I would like to set the sorting of the tables set to the SourceLogistics Area in the Picking List format to the "MaterialOutputPositionNumberText" criteria.

We have confirmed that the relevant setting exists in "Business Configuration", and we have already confirmed that the setting works only on the basis of the SourceLogistics Area.

However, the customer wants a table sorted by "MaterialOutputPositionNumberText".


Please check if there is no way to modify the sorting method.

 

Regards,

Administrator


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: [BYD] Modify Picking List Sorting criteria
entry.content:
Dear Administrator,

 

I understand your query and after reviewing the information, it appears that the system's sorting criteria for the pick list is based solely on the Logistics Area, as stated in KBA 2505137 - Pick List Sorting. This means it might not meet your specific requirements. I appreciate your understanding on this matter. If you have any further questions or need additional assistance.

 

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: Re: BTE for parked document
entry.content: Hello Raymond, Thank you for your answer. I got your point.

entry.content: This is complete encyclopedia of SAP S/4 HANA journey. Thanks for sharing it.

entry.title: Re: Logs to check who deployed/undeployed the iFlow
entry.content: Thank you, Suelen, I already checked the Audit Log Viewer and this information is missing. I also checked the logs in the Cloud ALM Monitoring. This information is not only missing in CPI monitor but in all logs anywhere.

entry.title: CPI Alert Notification Template in Groovy
entry.content:
This is a simple one. I just developed a simple program for generating alert notification mail body in HTML catching the exception raised by the Cloud Integration Framwork. 

You need to configure a separated mail receiver for pushing the content into whoever concerns. 🙂

One of the benifit of using this piece of code is that , the only input parameter is: mail-receiver.

or if you do not want to even configure it , you can put the receiver in the value mapping

valueMapApi.getMappedValue('CPI', 'cpi:office365:receiver', 'receiver', 'OFFICE365', 'cpi:office365:receiver')

Please read the code carefully before using it and you take your own responsibility for the consequence. 🙂

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import org.apache.camel.Exchange
import org.apache.camel.builder.SimpleBuilder

import java.time.Instant
import java.time.ZoneId
import java.time.ZonedDateTime

import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory
import com.sap.it.api.mapping.ValueMappingApi

/* ************************************************************************
    Program     : AlertNotification.groovy
    Create Date : Mar-26-2025
    Author      : Stephen Xue
    Parameters  :
        message --> message reference from framework;
        testFlag--> true for test mode; default for production code mode;
    Function    :
        1.
    Source: HTTP Header alert-receiver
    Target: HTML
 *************************************************************************/
Message processData(Message message)
{
    def map       = message.getProperties();
    def msgID     = map.get("SAP_MessageProcessingLogID");
    def receivers = map.get("alert-receiver");;
    def exceptionMessage, exceptionCode,exceptionName, tenantType;
    
    // Tenant type: prod or non-prod
    def tenant = System.getenv("TENANT_NAME");
    if(tenant.contains('-non-')){
        tenantType = 'Non-Prod';
    }else{
        tenantType = 'Prod';
    }    
    
    // Get receiver from ValueMappingApi
    if(receivers == "" || receivers == null ){
        def valueMapApi = ITApiFactory.getApi(ValueMappingApi.class, null);
		try{
			receivers = valueMapApi.getMappedValue('CPI', 'cpi:office365:receiver', 'receiver', 'OFFICE365', 'cpi:office365:receiver');
		}catch(Exception e){
			receivers = "yourusername@example.com";
		}
    }
    message.setHeader('mail-receiver',receivers);

    // Get iflow name and subject
    Exchange exchange = message.exchange;
    def evaluateSimple = { simpleExpression ->
        SimpleBuilder.simple(simpleExpression).evaluate(exchange, String);
    }
    def iflowName = evaluateSimple('${camelId}');
    def subject = "[${tenantType}]iFlow: '${iflowName}' has error";

    // Get system time
    Instant instant   = Instant.now();
    ZoneId zoneId     = ZoneId.of( "Australia/Melbourne" );
    ZonedDateTime zdt = instant.atZone( zoneId );
    def systemTime = zdt.toString();

    // Get Message Link
    String url = System.getenv("TENANT_NAME")+"."+System.getenv("IT_SYSTEM_ID")+"."+System.getenv("IT_TENANT_UX_DOMAIN");
    def msgIDLink = "https://"+url+":443/itspaces/shell/monitoring/MessageDetails/%7B%22messageGuid%22%3A%22"+msgID+"%22%7D";
    def ex = map.get("CamelExceptionCaught");
    if (ex!=null)
    {   // Exception Handling in OData V2 Receiver Adapter
        if(ex.getClass().getCanonicalName().equals("com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.OsciException")){
            exceptionMessage = ex.getMessage();
            exceptionCode    = message.getHeaders().get("CamelHttpResponseCode").toString();
            exceptionName    = ex.getClass().getCanonicalName();
        // Exception Handling in HTTP Receiver
        }else if(ex.getClass().getCanonicalName().equals("org.apache.camel.component.ahc.AhcOperationFailedException")){
            exceptionMessage = ex.getResponseBody();
            exceptionCode    = message.getHeaders().get("CamelHttpResponseCode").toString();
            exceptionName    = ex.getClass().getCanonicalName();
        }else{
            exceptionMessage = ex.getMessage();
            exceptionCode    = message.getHeaders().get("CamelHttpResponseCode").toString();
            exceptionName    = ex.getClass().getCanonicalName();
        }

        mailBody =  "<!DOCTYPE html>\n" +
                "<html lang=\"en\">\n" +
                "<head>\n" +
                "  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />\n" +
                "  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\" />\n" +
                "  <title>EMAIL</title>\n" +
                "  <style>\n" +
                "table, th, td {\n" +
                "  border: 1px solid black;\n" +
                "}\n" +
                "</style>\n" +
                "</head>\n" +
                "<body>\n" +
                "<p>Hi,<br><br>\n" +
                "Please find below details for Interface Message failure.<br>\n" +
                "<table>\n" +
                "  <tr>\n" +
                "    <td><span style=\"font-weight:bold\">Tenant Type</span></td>\n" +
                "    <td>${tenantType}</td>\n" +
                "  </tr>\n" +
                "  <tr>\n" +
                "    <td><span style=\"font-weight:bold\">iFlow Name</span></td>\n" +
                "    <td>${iflowName}</td>\n" +
                "  </tr>\n" +
                "  <tr>\n" +
                "    <td><span style=\"font-weight:bold\">Message ID</span></td>\n" +
                "    <td><a href=\"${msgIDLink}\">${msgID}</a></td>\n" +
                "  </tr>\n" +
                "  <tr>\n" +
                "    <td><span style=\"font-weight:bold\">HTTP Status</span></td>\n" +
                "    <td>${exceptionCode}</td>\n" +
                "  </tr>\n" +
                "  <tr>\n" +
                "    <td><span style=\"font-weight:bold\">Timestamp</span></td>\n" +
                "    <td>${systemTime}</td>\n" +
                "  </tr>\n" +
                "  <tr>\n" +
                "    <td><span style=\"font-weight:bold\">Exception</span></td>\n" +
                "    <td>${exceptionName}</td>\n" +
                "  </tr>\n" +
                "  <tr>\n" +
                "    <td><span style=\"font-weight:bold\">Error Message</span></td>\n" +
                "    <td>${exceptionMessage}</td> \n" +
                "  </tr>\n" +
                "</table><br>\n" +
                "Do Not Reply to this mail. Please contact SAP-Integration Team for further investigation.<br><br>\n" +
                "Regards,<br>\n" +
                "SAP Integration Team.\n" +
                "</p>\n" +
                "</body>\n" +
                "</html>";
        message.setHeader('subject',subject);
        message.setBody(mailBody);
    }
    message.setHeader('mime-type','text/html');
    return message;
}

 This is how the alert notification looks like in my mail box.

feel free to modify the code and have fun.

entry.title: Re: ONNM - NNM1 Numbering Issue
entry.content: Que pena encontrarse con esta pregunta y que a pesar del tiempo aún no tenga una solución. Yo me encuentro en una situación similar con un cliente en estos momentos.

entry.title: Re: How can I deploy Fiori App with POST API call functio...
entry.content: Could you check if the tutorial can help you? Or you may need to create a new thread about the standard API you are using. https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-can-i-deploy-fiori-app-with-post-api-call-function-to-cloud-public/qaq-p/14083938#feedback-error

entry.title: PayPal Payment Button with SAP Build Workzone & S/4HANA Public Cloud via PayPal Braintree
entry.content:
We’ve developed a custom sales platform using SAP Build Workzone, integrated with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, and are in the process of implementing PayPal as a payment method.

We are currently using PayPal Braintree as our payment service provider (PSP), and the integration requires the use of the PayPal JavaScript SDK – https://developer.paypal.com/sdk/js/ – to render the PayPal payment button on the front end.

We’re encountering issues embedding/loading this PayPal JavaScript SDK within the Workzone environment. Anyone know of a workaround or how to load a JavaScript library on SAP Build?

The ultimate goal is: the user should be able to see the PayPal payment button on Checkout screen, and on click, it should trigger a new page to sign into their PayPal account and load the checkout details i.e. total price and allow them to choose and submit payment.

entry.title: Re: Using Excel SDK of SAP Build Process Automation to extract data from multiple spreadsheets
entry.content: Is there any way to extract Excel data without the Desktop Agent?

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 Product Release: Add a Note, View Notes, etc...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your feedback. 

The KBA3582369 - Missing custom blocks after Enabling Full Profile for the latest People Profile in the Company System and Logo Settings said: 

  • These block types are not in current migration scope for PPX: Notes Block, Tags Block, Live Profile User Information Block.

 

Currently I am confirming with internal senior team. Once I get final conclusion, will keep you posted. Thanks!

 

Best regards,

Hannah

entry.title: Re: Nested Post Calls in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
hi @YashminBehera, did you try to troubleshoot with approach given? again I would encourage you to try with postman / curl first. You should be able to post sales order items with the example API link given previously.
Regards.

entry.title: Retrieving the highest PurchaseOrderItem from CDS view
entry.content:
SAPでカスタムCDSビューを作成しています。
I_PurchaseOrderItemビューからPurchaseOrderItemフィールドの最大値を取得したい。
ただし、PurchaseOrderItem は文字列フィールド ("00010"、"00020" など) であり、数値フィールドではないため、MAX 関数の使用は期待どおりに機能しません。
MAX 関数または同様の集計を CDS ビューの文字列型フィールドに適用して、最大値を取得するにはどうすればよいですか?

entry.title: Display changed field records by 'Display Changes to Objects'?
entry.content:
Hello, everyone

I have activated Business Event Logging.

But when I change the relevant documents, I can only see my change time and document name, and cannot display which fields I have specifically changed, as well as their new and old values, which is inconsistent with the introduction of the app.

Do these fields need to be configured? How can I record field changes?

entry.title: Planned receipt of refurbished material appear in MD04 though REFB has CRTD status
entry.content:
Hello again everyone. I hope someone may help me with my question. 

So we have a REFB PM Order that has CRTD status. My question is why is it the planned receipt for the refurbished material appears in MD04 whereas the planned consumption for the material to be repaired doesn't appear in MD04? Is that standard behavior? For my testing, I used the same material as the to be repaired and repaired (refurbished) to test the scenario. The MRP Type of the material is set to PD = MRP. A reservation has been created for the material to be repaired but this does not appear in MD04 (I assume because the Mvt Allowed indicator is not yet checked). When I released the REFB order, that is when the planned consumption for the same material appeared in MD04.

May I ask please for some clarifications about this? Apologies in advance for the newbie question as I am still learning. Thank you in advance for the help and guidance 🙂

entry.title: Re: Integration of employee data to LMS
entry.content:
Hey @RafaelHernandez 

There are some really good resources out there to guide you through this with the best being an SAP Implementation Design Principle Document "SAP SuccessFactors Suite: Impact of Implementing Employee Central for LMS Customers" which i tried to find the latest version on line but couldn't (so ping me if you have no luck either and I'll send you a copy of the PDF I have).

Or there are multiple blogs showing you how to do it like this one:

SuccessFactors: Goodbye Provisioning Jobs Part 1 |... - SAP Community

I highly recommend using the Design Principle document as it shows you a lot of tips and tricks that the Implementation Consultants have learnt over the years and added to the one document.  Such as how to configure the connector import settings to get away from the need to have the double header in the import file etc...

Let me know how you get on.

Cheers

Nathan H.

SAP SuccessFactors LearningSAP SuccessFactors Employee Central 

#integration_Centre

entry.title: SG bank file format for upload
entry.content:
Hi support,

I would like to open again this issue which we haven't solved with the client.

I have few questions regarding the creation of Payment file structure in the system:
1. Since SG is a pre-localized country, may I know which of the standard Payment Medium format is applicable to Singapore banks?
2. Since we need to create our own Payment File structure, we are confronted with the below scenarios:
2.1. Details requires to select Product Type (3 char) info provided by the bank. How are we able to assign or encode this data manually if we are to pull this data in the Output file, if we have limited options/fields available during payment processing? What are your suggestions/ field to use, if banks will require something like this?
2.2. Details also requires Transaction codes (2 char) info provided by the bank. Same question as 2.1.
2.3 Purpose of Payment (4 char) info provided by the bank. Same issue as 2.1.

Yes we are able to generate the file, however because of the missing data mentioned, bank file is always rejected.

Please advise.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SG bank file format for upload
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your follow-up regarding the payment file generation for Singapore.

Please find below our inputs based on standard system capabilities:

1. Standard Payment Medium Format for Singapore
While Singapore is a pre-localized country in SAP Business ByDesign, no standard bank-specific Payment Medium Format (PMF) is currently delivered for SG banks. 

2. Data Requirements in Payment File Output
Regarding the additional data fields required by your bank:

2.1. Product Type (3 characters)
2.2. Transaction Code (2 characters)
2.3. Purpose of Payment (4 characters)

These fields are not available in standard payment processing screens or configurations, and there are no dedicated fields in the standard system to capture this information during payment proposal or execution.

As this involves a custom payment file structure and enhancements to standard behavior, 

If you need assistance implementing a solution to include these fields, we recommend reaching out to your implementation partner.

Thank Yoou,

really appreciated your help.

entry.title: SAP GUI Quartz Theme Issue
entry.content:
Some of our user are facing an issue with SAP GUI especially something to deal with Quartz Theme, below is the screenshot of their screen. This happens randomly but goes away once user logs out and restarts the GUI after few minutes. But it does come again after certain period of time. We have tried re-installing SAP GUI and cross checked all theme settings as well. Still unable to find the root cause or solution. We assume it is some issue related to memory or rendering. Would appreciate any advice to resolve this issue. Thank you.

SAP GUI Version:

Theme Settings:

entry.title: Re: choosing rows/columns from data source to show in Dat...
entry.content: That's right, hidden table should not impact story performance, but you need to test it out anyway.

entry.title: Re: SAP Landscape Management Cloud discontinued
entry.content:
SAP treats on-premise and self-hosted/managed public cloud providers the same way … basically you have a perpetual license of some type.  SAP considers “cloud” to only be SAP managed (eg GROW or RISE with SAP).

For self-managed environments you can continue to use LAMA until December 2027 (or December 2030 with extended/customer maintenance). After that you can either run LAMA outside of mainstream maintenance (not recommended) or utilise other 3rd party solutions or build or own.

For public cloud providers and can utilise a number of automation tools and/or SAP integrated solutions such as Azure Center for SAP solutions.

Cheers

Shaun

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 Product Release: Add a Note, View Notes, etc...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your feedback. Standard 'Notes' block is not supported in latest people profile. Please refer to the info as below: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/configuring-people-profile/blocks-in-legacy-people-profile-and-cards-in-latest-people-profile

Handbook : Configuring People Profile >> Blocks Currently Not Supported in Latest People Profile:

Notes: Currently, you can use Continuous Feedback of Continuous Performance Management, which provides similar functionality.

 

Best regards,

Hannah

entry.title: System standard Inventory Aging Crystal Report
entry.content:
Hi All

It is not possible to edit the System standard Inventory Aging Crystal Report Design to show Vendor Name from GRPO and summarize Quantity by Vendor right?

The Crystal Report Design consists of unknown Table Fields and look restrictive like Print Layout Design?

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Re: How to enable Certificate based Authentication with S...
entry.content:
Hi Sugapriya, 

SAP Commerce Cloud doesn't provide CA client certificate.

For inbound scenario, i.e. user/client connecting to commerce webservice calls, you need to follow the inbound guide, i.e.provide trusted CA client certificate for your client and imported to commerce cloud domain.

When a client tries to establish a connection with the Commerce system, it uses a client certificate that is issued by the same trusted CA whose certificate was previously imported. In addition, the Common Name (CN) in the certificate's subject field matches one of the allowed domains.

Once the client presents its certificate, the Commerce system verifies the client's certificate against the imported trusted CA certificate to ensure it is valid and trustworthy. 

See Trusted CA Certificates for Client Authentication.

And there is no other code/configuration change needed to validate the certificate. Validation will happen when client connects to Commerce Cloud domain for corresponding request.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance, SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi @Nikhil1905 

The planning categories we have in Enterprise Projects – revenue projects are all manually uploaded (there are no plans that are automatically derived out of logistic data like time, material costs etc.). So the customer would have to upload and maintain all these versions. Project Cost Report provides the end user with comparison of any 2 planned categories along with commitment, actual costs and project stock costs. Please check the screenshot below. 

In addition, we have delivered a new project financial booklet - professional services in 2502. In the booklet, there is a page group for enterprise projects. Currently users can do selection based on the plan categories like budget, plan and manual plan category. However, the comparison of different plan versions with actuals are not covered yet. We can consider the request as future improvement for the project financial booklet for enterprise projects. Regarding quantities, it would be good to understand what should be shown. Is it only required for actuals? It would be good if you can share an example report. Please feel free to reach out to me if you have further details on this. 

Thanks and best regards,

Chenchu

entry.title: Re: Measuring documents posting -reg
entry.content: Number of Entry List

entry.title: Re: Exciting Update: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training Courses are Moving to learning.sap.com!

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Mobile Application in SAP Build – "N...
entry.content:
@davidebruno 

In general, the mobile services entitlement should be included by default when signing up for a new trial account.

Could you please check and confirm the following:

  • Whether the mobile services entitlement is available in the trial subaccount (see attached screenshot1.png)
  • Whether the BindMobileApplicationRouteToME application in the Started state (see attached screenshot2.png)

Regards,
Jitendra

entry.title: 一部のサービスリクエストが表示されない
entry.content:
サービスリクエストは表示可能で、依頼も実施できるが、「Create Hyperscaler LB for INBOUND traffic from an INTERNAL」のテンプレートが検索しても表示されず、作業依頼ができない。権限が不足していると思われるが、何の権限を付与すればいいのか教えて欲しい。


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 一部のサービスリクエストが表示されない
entry.content:
本事象は、権限不足により発生していたと推察されます。

サービスリクエストアプリケーションにてすべてのテンプレートを表示するには、
Sユーザに「Create Billable Service Requests」の権限が顧客番号またはCCCレベルで付与されている必要があります。なお、現在ご利用のSユーザには、インストール番号レベルで割り当てられていることを確認しております。 このため、「Create Hyperscaler LB for INBOUND traffic from an INTERNAL」のテンプレートが検索しても表示されず、作業依頼ができない事象が発生していると考えられます。

本来、必要な権限を持つ貴社管理者の方に権限依頼を行っていただく必要がございますが、本事象を早急に解決するため、弊社バックエンド上で必要な権限を付与させていただきました。 
お手数ですが、お手すきの際に事象に変化がないかご確認いただけますようお願いいたします。

詳細について記載されているKBAをお送りいたしますので、ご参考になれば幸いです。

2720477 - How to access Private Cloud Service Requests - SAP for Me 
3463116 - Missing template(s) when creating service request - SAP for Me  

上記内容について何かご質問や懸念事項がございましたら、お気軽にご連絡ください。

SAPサポート
Judy

entry.title: First Half 2025 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits?
entry.content:
At SAP, innovation isn’t just a one-time event—it’s an ongoing commitment. We’re constantly evolving our solutions to help organizations deliver exceptional employee experiences that meet the needs of today’s workforce.

A Quick Look Back

In the First Half 2024 Release, we introduced a new guided benefits enrollment experience —designed to simplify and streamline the process for employees. From the start, this intuitive, user-friendly approach was met with enthusiasm by existing customers and hailed as a game-changer by those evaluating SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits. The refreshed enrollment experience makes benefits enrollment more seamless, simple and intuitive for employees. 

 Refreshed Benefits Enrollment Experience (Desktop)

With the Second Half 2024 Release, we introduced a refreshed benefits overview page for insurance, savings, and pension benefit types. This new experience, available on desktop and mobile devices, provides employes with a single place to view their benefits details and take action such as declaring a life event, managing their beneficiaries, or enrolling in benefits. And, with convenient access from the ‘Manage My Benefits’ quick action card on the home page, the benefits overview enables employees to easily find a summary of their enrolled benefits, view their confirmation statement, and see available benefits.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page (Desktop)

The updated Benefits Overview page consolidates benefits activities that were previously distributed across the employee profile and legacy benefits overview. As part of the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile application, the new benefits overview provides employees access to all the features of the desktop experience from the convenience of their mobile devices.

Refreshed Benefits Overview Page (Mobile) 

What’s New in the First Half 2025 Release

Building on the momentum of last year’s innovations, the First Half 2025 Release brings exciting new enhancements that continue to elevate the global benefits experience. Our focus remains on making it easier for employees to not only enroll in their benefits—but also to manage and truly understand them.

Deductible Allowance on the Refreshed Benefits Overview Page

Employees can now take advantage of an expanded benefits offering that reflects the diverse needs of today’s workforce. With this release, we continue to enhance the benefits experience with new capabilities to help ensure all customers can take advantage of the recent innovations. With support for two additional benefit types, customers can now use the latest benefits experience for deductible allowances and other benefits such as an onsite gym, parking or cafeteria.

Other Benefit Type in the Refreshed Benefits Experience

These are just a few of the many innovations available with the SAP SuccessFactors First Half 2025 Release. To learn more, check out the resources below:

  • Visit the SAP SuccessFactors Community for release documentation, product videos, and solution area blogs
  • Watch the 1H 2025 Release Highlights Overview Video, featuring Bianka Woelke, GVP Head of Application Product Management

Dear Jeremy，

Thank you very much for your detailed explanation. However, I have tried several times but still failed to find this 'Step Setting'. But the 'Step Setting' still doesn't show up in the step condition.

entry.title: Inbound Delivery 180000013 could not be distributed Message No. VL589
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

While saving inbound delivery in S4 system I am getting the message: ‘Inbound Delivery XXXXXX could not be distributed Message No. VL589’.

I have checked in VL32N -->Edit-->check distribution document and it tells me: 'The delivery cannot be distributed (see the subsequent message) Message No. VL452'.

I wonder the interface between ECC and EWM is not well configured.

Has anyone had similar experience and got the accepted solution?

Thanks a lot!

Vincent

entry.title: Re: The SAP BW Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud

I was looking into SAP Note : 3590400 , to configure  SAP BW Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud , will it be managed by SAP or customers are expected to follow the steps as needed. I am specifically looking for the steps which might not be explicitly mentioned in the said note. Could you pls. help me here. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: DMEEX One format tree for multiple XML files
entry.content: Thank you for your insights. What works for me is when I added FPAYH-ZBUKR and FPAYH-RZAWE in the sort/key fields

entry.title: Re: CAPACITY REQUIREMENT LEVEL PERSONNEL NUMBER (ASSIGNME...
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for raising these concerns. The behavior you’re experiencing with Assignment Type 4 in SAP Work Manager—such as inactive Start and Pause buttons and issues with work order transfer—can stem from specific limitations and configuration requirements tied to this assignment type.

Let me address your questions point by point:

1. Inactive Start/Pause Buttons with Assignment Type 4

Yes, this is a known challenge. Assignment Type 4 (based on Capacity Requirement/Personnel Number) does not always interact cleanly with the UI action states out-of-the-box. In most cases, the start and pause button behaviors are tied to operation-level assignments, and if these are not fully aligned during configuration, the app UI does not update as expected.

2. Enhancements Required?

Absolutely. Many implementations using Assignment Type 4 require custom enhancements or BAdI implementations to handle status updates and synchronization events properly. You might need to adjust the mobile data object configurations and ensure correct BAPI mappings for time confirmations and status management.

3. Work Order Transfer Failing During Sync

This could be due to inconsistencies in how work orders are assigned or ownership conflicts in the back-end. Check your sync rules and ensure the operations are assigned to the exact personnel number linked to the mobile user.

4. Time Confirmation Inactive for Started Orders

This is again tied to the status management configuration in the mobile app. You’ll need to confirm that status-dependent UI rules are correctly configured and that any workflows or enhancements recognize the Assignment Type 4 logic.

🔧 OSS Notes and Resources

There are OSS Notes related to these issues, depending on your Work Manager version. Some commonly referenced ones include:

  • OSS Note 2094343 – Configuration guidelines for Assignment Type 4

  • OSS Note 2366630 – Troubleshooting Work Manager sync and status issues

  • You can search the SAP ONE Support Launchpad for updated OSS Notes specific to your version: SAP Support Launchpad

✅ Final Suggestion

If you're also involved in change management or need help structuring technical assignments or reporting, consider using CMI Level 4 Assignment Help for expert support on leadership and process documentation that aligns with frameworks like SAP implementations.

Let me know if you’d like help locating more OSS Notes based on your exact SAP Work Manager version.

Best regards,
[CMI Assignment Helper UK ]

entry.title: Crystal Report Runtime License
entry.content:
We are using Crystal Report for Visual Studio 2022 to develop a web base application solely for a customer and application is an internal application.

According to SAP Crystal Solutions FAQ | SAP Community, a little bit confusing.  Does the red circled block below apply to desktop app and server (thin) client deployment only, and the green circled block apply to web based application server side deployment?  If so, we need to buy runtime license for our case?

entry.title: Re: RELEASE STRATEGY PURCHASE REQUISITION
entry.content:
Hi Expert

Thank you very much,

I followed the recommendation, however, in the CL24N you get an inconsistency message, apparently that happens because the feature has no values. So, I added two values in the feature values tab:

Empty - No contract

    *       - With contract

Then in the CL24N for the new feature I checked the value (* No contract) and it worked. Please let me know if the solution I applied is ok or there is another way.

Thanks Percy

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Credit Management Configuration Process
entry.content: Very Useful Information

entry.title: Reusing Story Reports Across Annual Compensation Plan Templates
entry.content:
Hi everyone

I'm looking for guidance on how to efficiently reuse Story Reports for Compensation Planning across yearly review cycles in SAP SuccessFactors.

Each year, we copy the Compensation Planning template for the new compensation cycle (e.g., from "2024 Compensation Review" to "2025 Compensation Review"). We also have a budget overview report, following Learn How to Create A Compensation Budget Dashboard in Report Stories  

The challenge is that Compensation Planning Story Reports are template-specific. There is no option to switch the data source to a new template, and no support for referencing “all templates” or a generic dataset. As a result, we need to manually rebuild the report each year — including recreating queries, tables, and charts — even though the structure is identical.

What we’ve tried:

Saving the Story Report as a template: layout is retained, but queries must be rebuilt from scratch.

Exporting the report: results in package files, and no way to edit

Attempted to edit the query or switch the data source: not possible within current capabilities.

Question: Is there a supported method or workaround to reuse a Story Report across multiple, structurally identical Compensation templates (e.g., annual cycles)? Any insights or approaches would be appreciated, @xavierlegarrec  

Thanks. 

James Vu

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 HANA Job Service needs to be restarted everyday otherwise emails are not sent
entry.content:
Hi Ankit

We are facing this issue for 2405 patch also.

Please help!

Thank You!

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/getting-started-with-abap-cloud-development

Register here For doubt clearing or Queries 1 Hr.

entry.title: Re: EWM Outbound delivery - how to update a custom status value?
entry.content:
ah so, ok, thanks. I was to lazzy to check the customizing, but maybe it is a part of the status customizing, and maybe standard have it just customized this way, if so, you need to customize activities (business transactions) which would switch your statuses. https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/configuring-user-status-management/ba-p/13164016

I customized own statuses for HU in EWM, and there we switched it with FM STATUS_CHANGE_FOR_ACTIVITY or something similar.

For HU/Storage bin it is usual case, because with status you can prohibit business transactions (like deletion of the HU, or movement of the stock). And like I said if such logic not required it is easier with EEW 🙂

Sanjay

entry.title: Recruiting Agency Postings
entry.content:
We have requirements from clients where they need Agencies to send more data than what is available in the agency portal:

  1. Can the fields on the submit candidate page in the agency portal be customized?

2. If internal and external job descriptions differ, which job description does the agency posting take? Can Recruiter select which job description to share with which agency?

3. Lastly, does the job have to be posted externally, internally, or both for the job posting to be shared with an agency?

entry.title: Question with the configuration in /sdf/alm_setup
entry.content:
We are trying to configure different CALM use cases for ABAP systems via trsaction /sdf/alm_setup.

In the past we configured signavio and the target alm description shows signavio connection by default.
I can change and configure new Target ALM connection , but my question is , if this impact current Signavio connection by any change ?

Also, I will different use cases like Health Monitoring, Job Monitoring, Change and Deployment etc.
Do I have to configure Target ALM connection for each use cases or one connection is the recommendation from SAP
if the batch user have correct roles assigned for the required use cases?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Question with the configuration in /sdf/alm_setup
entry.content:
1. The existing Signavio Scenario won't be affected by creating a new connection for the SAP Cloud ALM.
    What is more, since ST-PI 740 SP27, the Signavio Process Insight Scenario can be configured within the transaction  /n/SDF/PINS_SETUP, please review the guide:
    https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-insights/administration-guide/connecting-system-directly

  2. It is recommended to use one connection for all the use cases.

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Hi, could you provide bit more information about this issue. Does it happen with preview app or standalone web build or both? You are using 4.12.236 version, right? In addition would be great to understand if this was working earlier in the week since we provided the patch? We haven't done any other updates since the patch early in the week

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 19: BTP Directory and Sub Account naming conventions
entry.content:
This video explains the importance of implementing clear and organized naming conventions for BTP Sub Accounts and Directories. It outlines a system starting with sequence numbers for sorting and identifying accounts, followed by acronyms to define their purpose. The script details naming conventions for directories, sub-accounts, subdomains, and Cloud Foundry spaces, emphasizing how these conventions improve clarity and help avoid confusion in system navigation. By adhering to these conventions, users can easily identify and manage accounts, making their directory structure clean and efficient for better operational efficiency.

 

Takeaways

  • Clear naming conventions are essential for easy identification of BTP Sub Acounts and Directories.
  • Bad naming practices, like using generic names (e.g., 'development'), can cause confusion in distinguishing different systems.
  • A good directory naming convention starts with a sequence number, followed by a descriptive name, and ends with the acronym in brackets.
  • The sequence number in directory names ensures proper sorting and organization of accounts and directories.
  • Sub Account names begin with the directory's sequence number, followed by a unique identifier based on the account's function (e.g., 'sandbox', 'development', 'production').
  • It’s important to use the same sequence number for related Sub Accounts, maintaining consistent organization across systems.
  • Subdomain URLs should omit sequence numbers to avoid clutter but still contain identifiers to link them to the correct directory and sub account.
  • Company acronyms should be added to subdomain names to ensure uniqueness and prevent overlap when using the same naming conventions across multiple clients.
  • For Cloud Foundry spaces, use simple names based on modules or projects, which reduces complexity in naming.
  • The naming conventions ensure that both directories and sub accounts are easily navigable, even in complex or flat lists, and help link super accounts to their corresponding directories.

entry.title: Re: How do I release a material that is in QI without an...
entry.content:
Greetings,

I have tried to replicate your scenario by doing a 561 movement to Q stock.

No inspection lot was created for this material since I did not activate the appropraite inspection type (in this example 05).

The question here is how was you colleague able to indicate that material should be posted to Q stock if no inspection type is active ("selected 'Post to stock' in the material master without identifying the inspection type") since this indicator is defined at inspection type level. Alternatevly you can select stock type while posting.

With movement type 321 you should be able to transfer quantities from Q stock to unrestricted  (or any other stock).

Bets regards, M.

entry.title: Re: In my BTP trial account trust configuration option mi...
entry.content: Hi. Are you doing this from a trial or free tier account? Thanks, Isidro.

entry.title: Can we generate service logic files in TypeScript with the help of Joule in Build Code
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I was exploring SAP Build Code , Can we use TypeScript for Application logic generation. 

By default its creating *.js files 
Thanks,

Satyajit

entry.title: Re: SAP PM - WCM, In the maintenance order level work app...
entry.content:
Greetings,

you need to assign the work permit to the reference object. When a maintenace order is cretae for this reference object the order inherits the permit which triggrs trhe ""WORK APPROVAL" button.

Alternatively you can assign a WCM default control key to the responsible work center in the reference object.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: ArchiveLink connection issue: ERROR => NiBufIConnect:...
entry.content: Hi @Aleksander, did you something on SAP side? Like in SMICM?

entry.title: Re: EWM Outbound delivery - how to update a custom status...
entry.content:
@Jelena_Perfiljeva-  unfortunately there was not mention that custom status would not be add there. 

Now I see hard code -

FYI- that method has the same code which you found in the example. Parameters are action code and Status...see the below example 

Here action_control = Activity = Your Custom Status (ZDOB) 

But standard and custom works differently. 

How standard works ? :

Assume Status Type : DWA in Outbound Delivery

 

SAP Standard DWA1 -> Not Started ( if you F4 on Status Value, you would see 0,1,2,9...etc)

However in custom Status :Example Initial D1T0 - Object value => X ( set because of Initial checkbox) 

"Additionally, I've also looked at the interface /SCDL/IF_AF_STATUS_MANAGEMENT because the name sounded promising. There is no documentation for it in SE24 but it is used in a report /SCWM/POST_PROC_DLV_SET_DOY, so I looked there for an example. The problem with this option is that it has "Activity" as a mandatory parameter and it is not relevant (or even available) in my case." => Again here in custom, Status Type, Status and activity are same. In your case, Activity would be ZDOB. And I think we have set 2 values '- ' or 'X' .

Please let us know if further questions...

PS: accepted that its bit tricky 🙂

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Field processing group not available in work order screen in SAP S/4.
entry.content: Dear Ken, I have the same issue, do you have informations about the pilot note - modification?

entry.title: Re: PO to BTP - IS Migration : Query parameters in HTTP r...
entry.content:
Hi

you can retrieve them via Groovy:
def lv_CamelHttp = lt_headers.get("CamelHttpQuery");

Regards
   Helmut

entry.title: Re: error in SFTP receiver channel in SAP 7.50
entry.content:
This error usually occurs if you have to authenticate using a certificate. This gets more and more common as this is more secure than ordinary basic authentication. Sometimes it's combined (Certificate + Basic)

If you can reach the SFTP from your local PC, try if you can connect via Filezilla (or any other SFTP client)

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: Planned receipt of refurbished material appear in MD0...
entry.content:
Greetings,

this might be due to reservation relevance indicator in component details screen. If it is not set to immediately this it will not be seen in MD04 in CRTD status but in (below example) in released status.

Bets regards, M.

entry.title: Re: How to handle generic JSON nodes with XCO?
entry.content: Thanks for the hint, I will give it a try. I am working on a BTP ABAP system and was using the XCO libraries because SAP recommends that for ABAP Cloud, as documented here: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/xco-library. But I will perhaps try use /UI2/CL_JSON instead, seeing as XCO does not seem to support such handling of JSON data.

entry.title: Two managers - same job: How to handle co-leadership in SuccessFactors?
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am working in an orgnaization with a lot of part-timers. We get increasingly more Managers who share their roles (e.g. 0.5FTE each). We struggle to find a manageable solution so both can approve workflows for their team or have visibility on the teams employee profiles.

Does anyone have a good idea on how to handle co-leadership in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  ?

Thanks

Andrea

entry.title: Re: Setting Up Programs in the LMS
entry.content:
When creating items, it's possible to select wether a learner should be able to self-enroll, if the manager should be able to enroll his/her employees, or if the item could only be assigned by admin.

It seems that with programs it's always possible to self-enroll, and managers can always enroll their employees. Is it possible to turn this off for programs?

Regards,

Merete

entry.title: Re: RELEASE STRATEGY PURCHASE REQUISITION
entry.content: hello, you dont need to put any characteristic values. In the first tab Basic data check Single value. Then you should be able to leave it empty in CL24N. However what you have choose may work as well.

entry.title: (RAP Application) - Using incoming value how to hide the entities (section) dynamically
entry.content:
Hi Developers,  

    My requirement is to conditionally hide sections based on a received value, but unfortunately. The solutions I have found so far are not adequate for my specific needs, and I'm continuing to search for a better solution.   

Help me out to overcome my issue   

Given the situation:
The system should dynamically manage the visibility of the partner entity during sales order creation, such that when the sales order type is one of 'MAKO', 'RTTC', or 'RTTR', the partner entity will be hidden from Application; conversely, for any other sales order type, the partner entity field must be shown to the user.

Found solutions:   

  1. Using Virtual Element in projection view  

         Some of the drawbacks include: 
            The entity (sections) cannot be hidden based on the incoming values. 
            Virtual element implementation will call only read the entity data not for modification face.  

     2. Control Fields with UI.Hidden Annotation  

  • Define Control Fields: Create control fields in your CDS view entity that determine the visibility of entities or sections.  
  • Bind to UI.Hidden: Use the UI.Hidden annotation and bind it to the control fields. This allows you to dynamically control visibility based on the values of these fields.  
  • Set Values Dynamically: Implement determination logic in your behavior definition to set the control field values based on incoming data.  

Some of the drawbacks include: 

  1. Unnecessarily create a field in CDS views. 
  2. Real-time customers won’t accept the dummy fields.  

       To illustrate:   

  1. Within my application, there are 10 sections, and in each section, I encounter difficulty in creating a field within my CDS view entity. Even though I managed to create 10 fields currently and they function as intended, there is a concern that future issues may arise once the application is handed over to the customer.  
  2. Because the customer wants to add another entity (Section) via the RAP Extension and simultaneously conceal a section contingent on a specific field's value, I cannot compel them to introduce an unnecessary placeholder field within their CDS view.   

 

Regards, 

Udhaya

entry.title: Re: In my BTP trial account trust configuration option mi...
entry.content: Sorry. 1-Provide: Microsoft Azure. Region: Singapore. 2-Provide: Amazon Web Services (AWS). Region: US East (VA). Do you see any other subaccounts in the Trial account? Thanks, Isidro.

entry.title: Offboarding not triggering for a new event reason
entry.content:
Hello

Has anyone ever had a case where offboarding does not trigger for a newly created event reason. We have granted the relevant permissions and added the event reason to the offboarding rule. But when we try to terminate an employee with that event reason the employee does not appear on the offboarding dashboard.

Kind Regards
Tumi Mumba

entry.content: No NSDM_V_MARC

entry.title: Re: An error occurred during the import of fixed asset po...
entry.content: Dear Jeremy, Your assistance was invaluable.I really appreciate your patient support! Without your expertise, I couldn't have solved the problem! I truly appreciate your help once again! Best regards,Yahaha

entry.title: Re: Bapi is is not working SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CREATE
entry.content:
What kind of answer do you expect to get, if you do not provide any detailed information about your condition type, your calculation schema, access control, potential user-exits or formulars, your process etc.

Is it possible, that your condition is taken from your referenced document if you create return order via VA01?

During creation via bapi no data is copied from a referenced document, e.g. sales order.

entry.title: Re: Issue in activating Extended CDS View
entry.content: nsdm_v_marc

entry.title: Re: Issue in activating Extended CDS View
entry.content: No NSDM_V_MARC

entry.title: Re: Issue in activating Extended CDS View
entry.content: Any suggestion will be highly appreciated

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP virtual field leads to out of memory
entry.content: Please make sure to set the virtual field(s) in your event handler in every case (not only conditiuonal) to a value.

entry.title: Re: In my BTP trial account trust configuration option mi...
entry.content: I'm using trial account with US East

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline Concept features: Supporting service interface operations and others
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

First of all thanks for the pipeline concept, overall it's great and really useful for us.

After updating the pipeline package to version 1.0.10 on our development system however, the Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for XI stopped working.

Every message we sent to the system directly ended up in the following error: DirectConsumerNotAvailableException: No consumers available on endpoint: direct://Sender.
 
 
All prerequisited were met, we tried deleting/redeploying etc but nothing helped. Eventually we created a case at SAP Support since we thought it might be a problem with our tentant. With their assistance, it turned out that the dynamic process direct receiver ${header.SAP_OutboundProcessingEndpoint} was causing this issue. The scenario we implemented did not follow this path so the header was never set (our scenario is/was quality of service EO with multiple receivers via XSLT). By configuring the partner directory to one receiver the error went away, and the iFlow was working again. After that we reconfigured to multiple receivers and removed the single receiver config. After doing this, the iFlow kept functioning as expected.

Our contact from SAP support however, explained that this error will reappear after every update + redeployment of the iFlow:

Dear Customer,

Everytime the iFlow is redeployed, which is everytime there is a update to the tenant/manually redeployed.

These rules come with the upgraded Apache version.

Hence it is better to maintain this header as a dummy value inside iFlow.

Is this something maybe you can consider doing in one of your future updates of the package? Setting the header with a default value so the error will be prevented? We can ofcourse adjust the standard ourselves, but we prefer to use the standard and benefit from future updates.

Many thanks for your response in advance.

Kind regards,
Arjan Nieuwenhuizen
